source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
<image_1> In the transition-state structure shown in the image, calculate the total number of bonds in the structure, including single, double, and triple bonds but excluding those involving hydrogen.

Note: Disregard arrows. Consider all components present in the transition-state structure shown in the image.
extraction_notes: `image_1` is present only as an in-memory PIL image marker (`<PIL.Image ...>`), not a usable path/URL/filename.

issues: metadata.extraction_notes states `image_1` was only present as an in-memory PIL image marker during source intake, not a usable source path/URL/filename.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly refers to the transition-state structure "shown in the image"
the requested quantity depends on visually identifying and counting bonds in the depicted chemical structure
multi_step: the task requires aggregating bonds across all components present in the transition-state structure
the prompt requires distinguishing bond types (single, double, triple) when counting total bonds
the prompt adds exclusion/filter rules: exclude bonds involving hydrogen and disregard arrows
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single explicit value
the counting criterion is well specified: include all components, count bonds, exclude H-involving bonds, ignore arrows
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true and a concrete image asset is present in image_manifest

risk_flags: metadata.extraction_notes states `image_1` was only present as an in-memory PIL image marker during source intake, not a usable source path/URL/filename.

decision_reasons: question text, answer, and a concrete image asset are all present, so the core semantics needed to recover the sample are available
initial scoring shows high image dependency and high verifiability, and the counting criterion is specific enough to support validation
the noted issues concern missing source-image metadata provenance rather than absence of the usable image itself, so they do not block candidacy
